titlestmt.titleproper: American Federation of Musicians (AFM) Local 767 records
Finding aid for the American Federation of Musicians (AFM) Local 767 records LSC.2341
titlestmt.author: Finding aid was prepared by Sadie Baldwin in the Center for Primary Research Training (CFPRT), under the supervision of Emily Wittenberg, 2026.

p.date: Online finding aid last updated 2026 March 13.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

publicationstmt.p: Finding aid description and metadata in this record are licensed under a Creative Commons Public Domain Designation (CC0 1.0 Universal). See LSC's Metadata Rights Statement for more information.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-04-24 11:10:52 -0700.

did.unittitle: American Federation of Musicians Local 767 records

origination.label: Creator
origination.corpname: American Federation of Musicians of the United States and Canada. Local 767 (Los Angeles, Calif.)

origination.label: source
origination.corpname: American Federation of Musicians of the United States and of Canada. Local 47 (Los Angeles, Calif.)

did.abstract: The American Federation of Musicians (AFM) Local 767 records document the organizational, financial, and representational activities of a Los Angeles musicians' union local from 1920 to 1965, with the bulk of the collection spanning 1920 to 1953. Covering the Local's early development through its later administrative operations, the collection includes records relating to the amalgamation of Local 767 with Local 47. Materials include general and board meeting minutes, election and membership records, incoming and outgoing correspondence, financial ledgers and statements, payroll and tax records, performer and institutional contracts, municipal and recording project files, wage and regulatory materials, and claims, disciplinary, and unfair list files. The collection documents the governance of Local 767 and its role in managing labor relations, member services, employment matters, and disputes within the Los Angeles music industry.
did.physloc: Stored off-site. Advance notice is required for access to the collection. All requests to access special collections material must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

Records in Series 3 were reviewed during processing for privacy concerns related to Social Security numbers; most of the named subjects were determined to be deceased, though a small number could not be conclusively verified.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Reproduction and Use
userestrict.p: Property rights to the objects belong to UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from the American Federation of Musicians Local 767, 2017.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], American Federation of Musicians Local 767 records (Collection 2341). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Processed by Sadie Baldwin, in the Center for Primary Research and Training (CFPRT), under the supervision of Emily Wittenberg, 2026.

Portions of the collection were re-housed for preservation purposes, including the disassembly of binders and bound volumes. Original order was preserved where discernible and original folder titles were retained where present and where they accurately reflected the contents. In some cases, titles were supplied, revised, or standardized for clarity, consistency, and description.

Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Problematic Content and Description in UCLA's library collections and archives.

bioghist.head: Administrative History
bioghist.p: The American Federation of Musicians Union Local 767 was a Los Angeles-based local of the American Federation of Musicians (AFM), the labor union representing professional instrumental musicians in the United States and Canada. Like other AFM locals, Local 767 represented its members in matters relating to employment, wages, contracts, benefits, membership, and professional conduct. Its activities included conducting general membership and board meetings, administering elections, maintaining membership and financial records, overseeing payroll and tax reporting, managing contracts and performance-related work, and pursuing claims, grievances, and disciplinary matters involving members and employers. The records also document regular correspondence between Local 767 and officers of the national federation, as well as with municipal agencies, labor organizations, and entities in the entertainment industry.

Local 767 operated during a period when the AFM maintained racially segregated local unions. In Los Angeles, Black musicians belonged to Local 767, while white musicians belonged to Local 47. In 1953, Los Angeles became the first AFM city in which separate Black and white locals merged, when Local 767 amalgamated with Local 47. This merger marked an important moment in the integration of AFM locals and anticipated broader desegregation efforts within the federation in the years that followed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection consists of administrative records, correspondence, financial and payroll records, contracts, project files, claims files, and related materials accumulated and maintained by the American Federation of Musicians Union Local 767, a Los Angeles local of the American Federation of Musicians. It documents the governance and operations of the local, including membership administration, elections, financial management, payroll and employment reporting, contractual activity, project work, and disputes involving musicians, employers, venues, and affiliated organizations.

The collection contains minutes of general meetings and board of directors meetings, voter eligibility and membership status lists, election materials, notices, flyers, and general office files. Correspondence documents communication with union officers, labor organizations, municipal and government bodies, and music industry representatives, including Leo C. Cluesmann, James C. Petrillo, and Harry J. Steeper. Financial and payroll materials include day books, ledgers, financial statements, bank statements, office expense records, payroll books, employee records, tax and withholding forms, unemployment insurance materials, and other recordings relating to compensation, accounting, and reporting requirements.

Contracts and program records include performer contracts, agreements with businesses and public institutions, municipal band project files, recording and transcription fund materials, performance records, and materials relating to wage scales, rules, and agreements. Claims, disciplinary, and enforcement materials include case files, death claims, defaulters and unfair list materials, and records documenting disputes involving musicians, employers, venues, and related parties.
The collection does not document all activities of Local 767 in equal depth, and some records are undated or lack complete contextual information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Original folder titles and labels were generally retained where present, though materials were arranged into series and subseries during processing to improve access while preserving identifiable groupings where possible.

This collection has been arranged in the following series:
Series 1: Governance, Membership, and Administration
Subseries 1.1: Minutes and Proceedings
Subseries 1.2: Elections and Governing Documents
Subseries 1.3: Membership and Status Records
Subseries 1.4: General Administrative Correspondence and Office Files
Series 2: Financial Records (Non-Payroll)
Subseries 2.1: Cashbooks, Day Books, and Ledgers
Subseries 2.2: Statements, Receipts, Disbursements, and General Accounting Files
Series 3: Payroll, Personnel, and Government Reporting
Subseries 3.1: Payroll Records and Payroll Systems
Subseries 3.2: Withholding/Tax Forms and Employment Compliance
Series 4: Contracts and Program Operations
Subseries 4.1: Member Engagement Contracts
Subseries 4.2: Municipal/Institutional Contracts
Subseries 4.3: Project Recommendations and Performance Administration
Subseries 4.4: Scales, Regulations, and Internal/Inter-member Agreements
Series 5: Claims, Disputes, Discipline, and Enforcement
Subseries 5.1: Claims and Case Files
Subseries 5.2: Death Claims and Supporting Documentation
Subseries 5.3: Defaulters / Unfair List and Disciplinary Matters

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Collections: Buddy Collette papers (2458). Available at UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.

Oral Histories: Interview of Buddy Collette [oral history transcript] / Steven L. Isoardi, interviewer. UCLA Library Special Collections, Center for Oral History Research interview, 1989. Available at UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
Interview of Florence Cadrez Brantley [oral history transcript] / Bette Yarbrough Cox, interviewer. Available at UCLA Ethnomusicology Archive.

controlaccess.subject: Jazz musicians -- California -- Los Angeles
Labor unions -- California -- Los Angeles -- Archives.
controlaccess.corpname: American Federation of Musicians of the United States and Canada. Local 767 (Los Angeles, Calif.) -- Archives
American Federation of Musicians of the United States and of Canada. Local 47 (Los Angeles, Calif.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents the governance, internal administration, and membership oversight of the organization. It includes minutes of general and board meetings, election-related records, membership status and eligibility lists, and administrative correspondence and office files. Together, these materials reflect the organization's leadership structure, internal decision-making, relations with officers of the American Federation of Musicians and other entities, and routine administrative operations.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into four subseries: Minutes and Proceedings; Elections and Governing Documents; Membership and Status Records; and General Administrative Correspondence and Office Files. Minutes are arranged chronologically; election and membership records are arranged by record type and date. Administrative correspondence and office files are grouped by function and correspondent, and thereunder generally chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains minutes of general membership meetings and meetings of the board of directors, spanning 1920 to 1953. Records are chiefly bound or formerly bound minute books, including some disassembled volumes. The minutes document organizational governance, motions, reports, elections, and other official business. Also includes supporting documents removed from one minutes volume.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

did.unittitle: Minutes, General Meetings and Board of Directors Meetings
Minutes, General Meetings and Board of Directors Meetings
Minutes, General Meetings and Board of Directors Meetings
Supporting documents removed from minutes volume
Minutes, General Meetings and Board of Directors Meetings
did.physdesc: Disassembled leather binder first half of Book I.

did.unittitle: Minutes, General Meetings and Board of Directors Meetings
did.physdesc: Disassembled leather binder second half of Book I.

did.unittitle: Minutes, General Meetings and Board of Directors Meetings
did.physdesc: Disassembled leather binder first half of Book II.

did.unittitle: Minutes, General Meetings and Board of Directors Meetings
did.physdesc: Disassembled leather binder second half of Book II.

did.unittitle: Minutes, General Meetings and Board of Directors Meetings
Minutes, General Meetings and Board of Directors Meetings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains records relating to internal elections and governance procedures, including election materials and election notices. These records document the nomination and election of officers and other internal electoral processes.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by record type and then chronologically. Multi-folder groupings are maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains records documenting membership standing, eligibility, and related status determinations. Materials include voter lists, suspension and reinstatement lists, member financial status lists, and other membership status records maintained for administrative reference. The records document how the organization tracked members' standing and rights within the Local.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by record type, with dated records filed chronologically where applicable. Undated reference and status lists are filed at the end of the subseries.

did.unittitle: Licensed recording and transcription companies - reference lists
Sub-Series 1.4: General Administrative Correspondence and Office Files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains general administrative files and correspondence relating to the organization's routine operations. It includes steward's reports, incoming telegrams, incoming and outgoing correspondence files, notices, flyers, return receipts, and commemorative materials. These records document day-to-day office administration, communication with union leadership and outside bodies, and other operational matters.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by record type and correspondent. Incoming and outgoing correspondence are grouped separately, then arranged by correspondent and then chronologically. Topical administrative files and general office materials are arranged by subject.

did.unittitle: Incoming correspondence from Leo C. Cluesmann
Incoming correspondence from Leo C. Cluesmann

Outgoing correspondence to Leo C. Cluesmann

Outgoing correspondence to Leo C. Cluesmann

Outgoing correspondence to Harry J. Steeper
Outgoing correspondence to Harry J. Steeper

Music Performance Trust Fund and Los Angeles City Council correspondence
Inventory of equipment from Local 767 and now being used by Local 47
War Manpower Referral Agency - Employer and Company Priority Lists

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents the organization's non-payroll financial activities and accounting practices. It includes day books, ledgers, financial statements, bank records, tax returns, bonds, refunds, loans, and related accounting files. The records reflect revenue and expenditures, dues and disbursement tracking, and broader financial administration apart from payroll and personnel-related reporting.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into two subseries: Cashbooks, Day Books, and Ledgers; and Statements, Receipts, Disbursements, and General Accounting Files. Record books are arranged by type and date. General accounting files are arranged by record type and then chronologically.

did.unittitle: Sub-Series 2.1: Cashbooks, Day Books, and Ledgers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains financial record books used to track receipts, disbursements, dues, and related accounting activity. Materials include day books, ledgers, and similar bound accounting volumes. These records document the organization's bookkeeping practices and provide summary evidence of financial transactions over time.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by volume type and then chronologically. Bound accounting volumes are maintained as discrete units.

did.unittitle: Sub-Series 2.2: Statements, Receipts, Disbursements, and General Accounting Files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains general accounting files and supporting financial documentation, including financial statements, bank statements, deposit and office expense records, tax returns, bonds, refund files, and records relating to loans. Together, these materials document the organization's broader financial administration, cash flow, liabilities, and accounting oversight.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by record type and then chronologically. Multi-folder monthly or annual groupings are maintained in sequence.

did.physdesc: Bound leather book and loose pages that were formerly bound.

did.unittitle: Fidelity and Deposit Co. bonds for Howard and Allen
Series 3: Payroll, Personnel, and Government Reporting

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents payroll administration, employee compensation tracking, and compliance with tax and employment reporting requirements. It includes payroll books and employee payroll records, tax analysis materials, withholding and unemployment forms, internal revenue reports, and other employment-related government reporting records.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. Records were reviewed during processing for privacy concerns related to Social Security numbers; most named individuals were determined to be deceased, though a small number could not be conclusively verified.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into two subseries: Payroll Records and Payroll Systems; and Withholding/Tax Forms and Employment Compliance. Payroll records are arranged by record type and date. Government reporting and compliance files are arranged by reporting type and then chronologically.

did.unittitle: Sub-Series 3.1: Payroll Records and Payroll Systems

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains records used to administer and track payroll, including payroll books, payroll employee records, tax analysis materials, and performance payroll documentation. These records document compensation systems and payroll processing for employees and performers. Some records contain Social Security numbers and other sensitive personal information, though most subjects have been determined to be deceased.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by record type and then chronologically. Alphabetical payroll employee files are grouped by year.

did.physdesc: Found tied together at back of [Payroll employee record, 1952] binder.
Found tied together at back of [Payroll employee record, 1952] binder.

did.unittitle: Sub-Series 3.2: Withholding/Tax Forms and Employment Compliance

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains tax, withholding, unemployment insurance, and employment compliance records, including internal revenue reports, withholding exemption certificates, unemployment records, Social Security materials, and state and federal reporting files. These records document the organization's compliance with government tax and labor requirements. Many files contain Social Security numbers and other sensitive personal information, though most subjects have been determined to be deceased.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by record type and then chronologically. Multi-folder filing units are maintained in sequence.

did.unittitle: California unemployment insurance: contribution return and report of wages
California State Department of Employment - taxes and audits

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents the organization's contractual activities and the administration of performance-related programs and agreements. It includes member engagement contracts, contracts with municipal and corporate entities, records relating to projects and performance administration, and files on wage scales, regulations, and internal agreements.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into four subseries: Member Engagement Contracts; Municipal/Institutional Contracts; Project Recommendations and Performance Administration; and Scales, Regulations, and Internal/Inter-member Agreements. Contract files are arranged alphabetically or by contracting entity. Project and administrative files are arranged by project or record type and then chronologically. Scale and agreement files are arranged by subject.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains contracts documenting engagements involving individual members, bands, and performers. The files are arranged alphabetically and include both general alphabetical contract groupings and individually identified performer files. These records document performance agreements, recording contracts, and other member-related engagements, and in some cases may contain sensitive personal information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by performer, bandleader, contracting party, or contract grouping. General alphabetical folders and individually named files are interfiled in alphabetical order. Multi-folder filing units are maintained in sequence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains contracts between the organization and municipal, corporate, or institutional bodies, including contracts with the City of Los Angeles and private companies.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by contracting entity. Multi-folder filing units are maintained in sequence.

did.unittitle: Contracts with the City of LA
Sub-Series 4.3: Project Recommendations and Performance Administration

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains records relating to the planning, recommendation, and administration of projects and performances. Materials include municipal band project files, studio orchestra managers' reports, performance worksheets, transmittal correspondence, and related administrative records. These files document how the organization coordinated, monitored, and reported on organized performance activity.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by project or record type and then chronologically. Multi-folder filing units are maintained in sequence.

did.unittitle: Recording and transcription fund project performance worksheets and transmittal correspondence
Sub-Series 4.4: Scales, Regulations, and Internal/Inter-member Agreements

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains records relating to wage scales, rules and regulations, and agreements among members or between the organization and employers or related unions. Materials include agreements between members, venue-specific scale agreements, memoranda of agreement, and files concerning motion picture and television scales and formulas. These records document the regulatory and negotiated framework governing work conditions and compensation.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by subject or agreement type.

did.unittitle: Lincoln Theatre wage scale price proposal
Memorandum of agreement - Million Dollar Theatre Inc., Hip Theatre Corporation, AF of M, and other unions
Motion pictures - scales, regulations, interpretations, contracts
Motion picture and television - 5 percent formula and fixed-fee correspondence and agreement
Series 5: Claims, Disputes, Discipline, and Enforcement

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents disputes, claims, disciplinary matters, and enforcement actions handled by the organization. It includes claims and case files, death claims, death certificates, defaulters and unfair list records, and misconduct-related files. These materials reflect the organization's role in adjudicating disputes, pursuing claims, enforcing agreements, and administering disciplinary and benefit-related matters.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into three subseries: Claims and Case Files; Death Claims and Supporting Documentation; and Defaulters/Unfair List and Disciplinary Matters. Claims files are arranged alphabetically by claimant, case name, or filing grouping. Death claim materials are arranged by record type. Disciplinary and enforcement files are arranged by subject.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains claims files and case records relating to disputes involving members, employers, venues, other organizations, and the federation. The files include alphabetically arranged claims as well as specifically identified case files. These records document grievances, financial and contractual disputes, internal decisions, and enforcement actions arising from performance and labor matters.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by claimant, case title, or alphabetical claim grouping. General alphabetical claim folders and individually titled case files are interfiled in alphabetical order. Multi-folder filing units are maintained in sequence.

did.unittitle: Bell, Aaron, vs. Cab Calloway, Case no. 205
Berry, Parker, vs. Warner Bros. Studio
Fain, Elmer, Hawthorne Club case: decision of the Internal Executive Board
Federation vs. Federal Artists and Edward Fishman
Federation, on behalf of Andy Kirk, vs. Club Congo / FAHM Enterprise (Fred Coleman and Evan Mosby)
Hollywood Four Blazes vs. Johnny Moore's Three Blazes
Johnson, Carl, vs. Bardu Ali and the Lincoln Theatre
Lutcher, Joe Orchestra, vs. Modern Music
Members vs. Excelsior Records, Otis René, and Gerald Wilson
Moore, Johnny (Three Blazes), vs. Charles Brown
Owens, Hilton, vs. Mrs. B. Reeves
Rivera, David, and John B. Letman, vs. Cab Calloway, Case no. 176
S and P vs. American Federation of Musicians, regarding Kid Ory contract and Billy Berg default
Young, Lee, and Dudley Brooks, vs. Cotton Club / Harold Stanley
Sub-Series 5.2: Death Claims and Supporting Documents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains records relating to death claims, including claim files and supporting documentation such as death certificates. These materials document the processing of death-related benefit or claim matters.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by record type.

did.unittitle: Sub-Series 5.3: Defaulters/Unfair List and Disciplinary Matters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains records relating to disciplinary actions, defaulters, unfair list matters, and misconduct cases. Materials include defaulters and unfair list records and correspondence relating to disciplinary agreements and conduct matters. These files document the organization's enforcement of standards, sanctions, and disciplinary procedures.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by subject.

did.unittitle: Four Spirits of Rhythm misconduct correspondence and conduct agreement regarding Hollywood Recreation Radio Room
